comment.body:
Monday: cautious risk-appetite, false breakouts, benign stabilization exists but tends to show up as reversal mechanics, not smooth trend.
Tuesday: slightly more benign, valuatino discrepancy, headline anxiety. Ranging.
Wednesday: data-reactive, but market is leaning towards positiveness anyway. Cautious risk-appetite.
Thursday: risk-off pivot, defensive rotation
Friday: looks like CPI will be forced to be interpretet as expansionary, building long-term risk into the markets. Reactions are expected to be exagerated.
comment.raw_body:
Monday: cautious risk-appetite, false breakouts, benign stabilization exists but tends to show up as reversal mechanics, not smooth trend.
Tuesday: slightly more benign, valuatino discrepancy, headline anxiety. Ranging.
Wednesday: data-reactive, but market is leaning towards positiveness anyway. Cautious risk-appetite.
Thursday: risk-off pivot, defensive rotation
Friday: looks like CPI will be forced to be interpretet as expansionary, building long-term risk into the markets. Reactions are expected to be exagerated.

comment.body:
"7004.41 - 7038.49 upside zone in case of vol crush that the market prices in, and
6789.12 - 6835.08 downside zone in case of vol up.
This is a 34.84% difference. Way lower than what the speed profile implies (82.57%). This high discrepancy tells me that the market expects a way thinner left-tail than what the actual convexity makes possible! And this is very dangerous, as vomma is negative until Friday. So the downside vomma zone is a major pivot that can catch many participants off-guard."

comment.body: wild, if accurate:  This data is incredible: 0DTE options now command a record ~78% of daily Nasdaq 100 volume (up 23 points since 2024) and ~65% of S&P 500 volume. They also account for nearly 60% of volume in $QQQ and $SPY, followed by $IWM at ~40%. Collectively, 0DTEs represent a record 55% of volume across these assets—up from 38% in 2024—signaling that risk appetite is through the roof.
